Aggregated bias · 1-glance synthesis
Today's verdict BULLISH-OPPORTUNISTIC
Reconciliation · why the tape contradicts itself
⚠ Signals disagree — Crash Index **2/10** (WATCH, calm price action) vs VIX Spike Tier **1/4** BROAD with **50%** probability and SKEW active
Spot tape is quiet while the options market is paying up for tail protection. Trust the options market here because SKEW leads realized stress and the divergence composite is already ELEVATED at **55.8%**.
⚠ Signals disagree — Regime State STABLE at **18** with MIXED bias vs Divergence composite ELEVATED, net bias **+12** bearish, **18** bearish pairs vs **6** bullish
Headline regime says nothing has broken yet, but the internals say correlations are reshuffling underneath. Trust the divergence read because correlation drift precedes regime breaks.
⚠ Signals disagree — Contrarian **75/100** STRONG BUY vs ^NDX **-3.2%** over 5 days as the global laggard and **0** active confluences
Sentiment says wash out and buy, but leadership is rolling over and no confluence has fired to confirm a bottom. Trust the contrarian signal only after one confluence activates; until then it is a setup, not a trigger.
Today's tension
Price action is calm but the options market and cross-asset correlations are screaming change.
Bull
Crash Index sits at **2/10** and the regime score is **18** with a STABLE label, so the underlying tape is not breaking. The Contrarian score at **75** flags capitulation washout conditions that historically reward buyers.
Bear
VIX Spike Detector is live at Tier **1/4** with a **50%** broad spike probability, the divergence composite is ELEVATED with **55.8%** of pairs diverging, and US large-cap is the world's laggard with **^NDX -3.2%** over 5 days.
The tell: Watch whether VIX backwardation activates and whether T1 stacks with HYG or B2. If a confluence flips on, the bear case wins; if SKEW resets and ^NDX stabilizes, the bull case wins.
AI read · Citrini · today's macro tape

The tape is calm on the surface and tense underneath, and that is the whole story today. Crash Index reads 2/10, regime state scores 18 with a STABLE label, and there are 0 active confluence triggers. At the same time the VIX Spike Detector is live at Tier 1/4 BROAD with a 50% probability, SKEW is active, the divergence composite is ELEVATED with 55.8% of pairs diverging, and the Contrarian gauge is already at 75/100 STRONG BUY. You have price stability sitting on top of options hedging demand and a correlation regime that is quietly reshuffling.

Position this as a barbell, not a bet. The contrarian washout score of 75 plus a stable regime argues for adding selective length in the names that have been punished, with ^NDX -3.2% over 5 days flagging US large-cap tech as the obvious bombed-out cohort. Hedge that length cheaply because SKEW is active and the 50% broad spike probability tells you tail insurance is bid for a reason. Keep gross modest until a confluence prints; with zero active triggers, you are paying for optionality, not chasing a confirmed move.

The main risk is that the options market is right and the spot tape catches down. The reading that flips you defensive is any T1 confluence activating, especially T1 x HYG (credit stress) or T1 x B2 (small-cap drawdown). If those go live alongside the existing SKEW signal, the contrarian buy is wrong and you cut risk fast. Until then, the calm score is your friend and the hedges are your insurance.

5-regime heuristic classifier · cross-asset
Macro Regime — where are we structurally
AI read · macro regime

The regime state is BROADENING with only 30% confidence, which is the macro version of "something is changing, but we are not sure what yet." Broadening means leadership is expanding past the mega-cap names that carried the tape, and that fits with ^RUT -0.5% outperforming ^NDX -3.2% in the US over 5 days. Low confidence means you size the thesis modestly; do not load up on a rotation trade the model is only 30% sure about. Position with a tilt toward equal-weight and small-caps over cap-weighted tech, keep hedges on via the active SKEW signal, and wait for confluence confirmation before committing harder. A broadening regime is bullish for breadth and bearish for concentrated mega-cap exposure.

Dow Theory generalized · cross-asset divergences
Cross-asset divergence radar — Cross-asset divergence watch
AI read · divergences in plain English

The cross-asset picture is the loudest signal on the board, full stop. The composite divergence zone is ELEVATED with 55.8% of 43 pairs diverging, net bias +12 bearish, 18 bearish pairs against 6 bullish, and the jaws score pegged at 100. In plain English, divergence means two assets that usually move together are no longer moving together, and correlation drift is how fast that relationship is changing. VIX vs MOVE flipped from -0.55 to +0.67, which is equity vol and rate vol now rising together instead of trading off. VIX3M vs VIX went from -0.34 to -0.82, deepening term-structure stress, and BTC vs SPY drifted from -0.71 to -0.29, meaning crypto is no longer the clean diversifier it was. When this many pairs are reshuffling at once, the market is rewiring its regime under your feet.

Cross-asset confluence · live status
Confluences — most proven alpha of the framework 0/3 ACTIVE
AI read · what the confluences mean now

Zero confluences are active, and that absence is the read. No T1 x HYG (vol stress meeting credit stress), no T1 x B2 (vol stress meeting small-cap stress), and no T1 x JPY (vol stress meeting safe-haven flow). The VIX spike signal is live on its own, but nothing else has joined it to confirm a real risk-off wave. You lean lighter on the bearish hedge case until at least one of these cross-checks fires. If T1 x HYG activates, credit is breaking and you cut risk; if T1 x JPY activates, global carry is unwinding and you go defensive. Right now, the spike detector is shouting into an empty room.

✅
T1 × HYG (VIX backwardation + Credit stress)
Volatility backwardation meeting credit stress — the highest-conviction pre-correction setup in the framework. Fires only when options fear and the bond market confirm each other.
STAND-BY

🟢 HYG/IEF stressZ=+0.95
✅
T1 × B2 (VIX backwardation + Small-cap drawdown)
Volatility backwardation with small-caps breaking down — breadth thinning while vol rises. A late-rally warning when the index masks weakness underneath.
STAND-BY
🟢 VIX3M/VIX backwardation1.030
🟢 IWM/SPY breakdownZ=+2.18
✅
T1 × JPY (VIX backwardation + Yen safe-haven flow)
Volatility backwardation with the yen bid — safe-haven flow confirming the vol signal. Classic risk-off rotation when carry trades unwind.

🌍
Asia · Europe · US · Crypto 24/7 — lead-lag detection
Cross-Region Surveillance
Major indices from the 4 regions/asset classes. Asia trades ahead time-wise vs US — a Tokyo overnight crash often leaks into the SPX open. Crypto = 24h leading indicator of global risk appetite.
AI read · the cross-region tape

The global tape is leaderless and the US is the weakest link. Asia is split with ^AXJO -0.8% holding up and ^KS11 -12.2% in a five-day collapse that screams local accident more than global signal. Europe is mixed, ^SSMI +1.9% leading on defensive Swiss flow while ^GDAXI -2.4% lags on cyclical weakness. The US is the standout problem, with ^RUT -0.5% as the regional leader and ^NDX -3.2% as the laggard, confirming the broadening regime read where small-caps relatively outperform tech. Crypto is the only risk asset bid, SOL-USD +7.3% and BTC-USD +4.0% over 5 days, which tells you risk appetite is not dead, it is rotating. Expect a US session that opens heavy on tech, with rotation flow into smaller names and any tech bounce sold until ^NDX stabilizes.

01
Forward-looking indicators · Bonferroni-validated
Crash Index — 10 lead-signal radar 2/10 · WATCH — 1-2 signals, monitoring
AI read · volatility & crash, reconciled

Crash Index at 2/10 and VIX Spike Tier 1/4 with 50% probability is a coherent split, not a glitch in the wiring. The Crash Index reads what already happened in price (drawdowns, breadth, credit), and right now none of that is broken. The VIX Spike Detector reads what the options market is paying for next, and SKEW being active means traders are bidding up out-of-the-money puts even while spot stays flat. You get this exact pattern before vol expansions: realized calm, implied tension, and a forward curve that has not yet inverted (backwardation is off). Trust the spike detector to lead and the crash index to confirm; if the score climbs above 3 with backwardation switching on, the calm broke.

CRASH INDEX
2/10
WATCH — 1-2 signals, monitoring
10 forward-looking indicators · Bonferroni-validated

lead 30-90d ❌ z=+1.36
DXY+SKEW combo structural
lead 45-346d ❌

lead 5-20d ❌ 1.03
STLFSI4 d20 stress
lead 5-30d ❌ -0.22
HYG/IEF z<-1.5 stress
lead 5-20d ❌ z=+0.95
T10Y2Y resteepen stress
recession warning ❌ +0.00
SPY -2% in 5d (breakdown) trigger
lead 5-15d 🔻 -2.55%
VIX +30% in 3d trigger
lead 1-10d ❌ +3.30%
Score 0-2 CALM · 3-4 WATCH · 5-6 WARNING · 7-8 ALARM · 9-10 EXTREME (rare crash imminent). Lead times: structural 30-90d · stress 5-30d · triggers 1-10d. Four Telegram alerts available depending on escalation.
How to read
Each row = an independent signal Bonferroni-validated over 15 years of SPY. The structural (DXY, SKEW, MOVE) signal a regime change over 30-90 days. The stress (HYG/IEF, VIX3M/VIX, STLFSI4) ring at 5-30 days. The triggers (SPY -2% in 5d, VIX +30% in 3d) are short-term breakouts. For a tactical decision: 3+ signals simultaneously aligned = real probability of stress, never on isolated signal (framework rule).
02

How to read
Score 0-100 aggregated from 6 sub-signals (each 0-20) that predict SPY underperformance. Bonferroni-validated over 15 years, walk-forward robust. Above 50 → active-bearish regime (trim winners, tight stops). DXY surge added in May 2026 (current regime 2023-2026, strong dollar = toxic for global risk-on).
03
Contrarian score · 3 buy signals (Bonferroni + walk-forward)
Contrarian Buy 75 · STRONG BUY — capitulation/inflation regime
75

Contrarian Score = signals that predict SPY OUTPERFORMANCE (capitulation buy). VIX backwardation → +7.29pp SPY at 120d · Corr SPY-TLT>0 → +3.88pp at 120d · SMH z<-1.5 → +11pp at 60d (test 2023-2026). Reading: score > 70 = aggressive fear-buy setup.
How to read
Bonferroni MCPT backtest 2026-04-30 over 15 years SPY · 9/15 tests VALID. Regime Score vs Contrarian Score are independent — they can coexist high (regime transition) or low (structural calm). Always read the combination, not each one alone.

Probability VIX +50% within 60d
50%
vs baseline 37% · backtested cycle 2023-2026
Action — sizing tied to tier
Watch (0-1% hedge book)
How to read
4 tiers (BROAD / MEDIUM / HIGH / EXTREME) based on the confluence of 3 Validated components: SKEW > 140 (tail-hedge demand), VIX3M/VIX > 1.05 (vol curve inverted), VIX < 16 + ratio > 1.15 (adaptive proxy). Each tier indicates a historical probability of VIX +50% at 60d (cycle backtest 2023-2026, vs baseline 37%). Strict sizing: tier 2 = 1-2% book hedges, tier 3 = 3-5% long-vol, tier 4 = 5-10% MAX (rare confluence).
05b
Under-the-hood vol · Observation (Imran Lakha)
Vol Microstructure — VIX beta & term structure
VIX spot
19.44
VIX beta 20d
-8.43
0th pct
VIX beta 60d
-2.91
0th pct
VIX9D / VIX
1.063
backwardation
VIX3M / VIX
1.102
contango
VVIX / VIX
5.18
vol-of-vol
VIX beta 20d collapsed (-8.43, 0th pct) — historically VIX becomes a buy. BUT only if VIX catches a bid on the next spot down-move. If VIX stays asleep, the setup is dead (Lakha's caveat).
How to read
VIX beta = how much VIX moves per unit SPX move (rolling regression). Collapsed beta (low pct) = VIX asleep → historically a buy, but worthless unless VIX re-engages on the downside. VIX9D/VIX > 1 & VIX3M/VIX < 1 = backwardation (stress). Observation only — no sized signal.

06
Aggregated bias · 53-ratio composite
Regime synthesis — RISK-ON / OFF / STRESS / TOPPING
MIXED — competing signals No clean composite per Kerberos. Wait for alignment.
RISK-ON aligned (3)
• Small caps leading
• Copper > Gold
• Industrials > Utilities
RISK-OFF aligned (1)
• ETH dumping vs BTC
STRESS aligned (0)
none
TOPPING aligned (1)
• SMH stretched extreme
How to read
Aggregation of all panel signals across the 4 cross-asset buckets. RISK-ON = risk appetite (cyclical, equity, EM). RISK-OFF = flight to defensives (TLT, gold, JPY). STRESS = liquidity tension (HYG, MOVE, STLFSI). TOPPING = end-of-cycle signals (concentration, breadth narrowing, defensives leading). Doctrine: a single signal = noise. 3+ aligned in one bucket = real confirmed regime.

07
VOL TERM STRUCTURE · 5 ratios
Volatility — VIX-family ratios
VIX3M / VIX
Stretch -1.04
Volatility term structure
1.0302 -3.95%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
This ratio compares 3-month fear (VIX3M) to immediate fear (VIX). Above 1, traders anticipate more stress in 3 months than today — that's the normal situation. Below 1, it's the opposite: immediate panic more expensive than long-term insurance. Current reading: 1.03 — normal calm regime. The curve is in typical contango, neither panic nor excessive complacency. Action: no directional signal, watch for a break below 1.00 (panic) or above 1.30 (top warning).
VVIX / VIX
Stretch -0.99
Vol of vol
4.868 +0.95%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
VVIX = volatility of the VIX itself. When it rises sharply, it means institutionals are actively buying insurance against a crash (deep puts, VIX calls). It's an internal stress signal from the options market. Current reading: Z=-0.99 — no particular stress in options. Average insurance level, neither complacency nor panic. Action: no directional reading, follow the other signals.
VIX9D / VIX
Stretch +2.76
Ultra short-term panic
1.1553 +3.68%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
VIX9D = 9-day fear, VIX = 30-day fear. When the 9-day exceeds the 30-day, it means an immediate shock is anticipated (often around an event: Fed, election, earnings). Current reading: 1.16 — VIX 9-day above 30-day, imminent shock priced. The market expects turbulence within the week. Action: don't chase rallies, keep cash to buy on the dip if the shock materializes.
SKEW
Stretch -0.04
Black swan insurance
143.1 +0.78%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
The SKEW index measures how much institutionals pay to protect against an extreme SPX crash (puts far out of the money, what's called 'tail-hedge'). Above 140, demand for protection is strong. Current reading: 143 — moderate demand zone for crash insurance. Big players are starting to hedge, no panic yet. Action: watch for a move above 150 = full alarm, otherwise stay neutral.
SKEW / VIX
Stretch -0.79
Tail-risk premium relative to vol
6.44 -9.88%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
SKEW/VIX compares the cost of extreme crash insurance to the cost of standard volatility insurance. When this ratio rises, it means tail fear is higher than daily fear — an interesting divergence. Current reading: Z=-0.79 — standard level, no gap. Tail insurance cost aligned with normal vol. Action: no signal.
VIX / Realized Vol 30d
Stretch +0.40
Vol regime persistence — chop vs crash detector
1.73 +4.54%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
Z=+0.40, last value 1.7330059584714324. No specific Kerberos reading defined for this pair.

CREDIT SPREADS · YIELD CURVE
Credit & rates — risk-on/off proxies
HYG / IEF
Stretch +0.95
High yield credit stress
0.8474 -0.10%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
HYG = high-yield bonds (junk bonds), IEF = US sovereign bonds 7-10 years. The ratio measures the health of risky credit: when HYG underperforms IEF, smart money is fleeing credit risk before it hits equities. Current reading: Z=+0.95 — balanced credit, no junk stress. The bond market is flagging nothing unusual. Action: no defensive reading from credit.
LQD / IEF
Stretch +1.40
Investment grade liquidity
1.1562 +0.16%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
LQD = investment-grade corporate bonds, IEF = 7-10y treasuries. When even quality bonds underperform treasuries, it means liquidity is tightening everywhere, not just on junk. Current reading: Z=+1.40 — quality credit stable. Investment-grade spreads healthy, no macro liquidity stress. Action: no defensive signal from quality credit.

TIP / TLT
Stretch -0.08
Inflation expectations
1.2747 -0.93%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
TIPS (inflation-indexed bonds) divided by TLT (long nominal bonds). When TIPS outperforms TLT, the bond market is pricing more inflation to come — risk for equity multiples. Current reading: Z=-0.08 — stable inflation expectations. No inflation panic priced. Action: no signal on multiples.
MOVE
Stretch -0.39
Bond volatility (BofA)
69.4 -6.09%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
MOVE = implied volatility of Treasury bonds (VIX equivalent for bonds). When the bond market becomes volatile, it's often a precursor to equity stress (bond vol leads equity vol). Current reading: 69 — normal bond vol. No Treasury market stress. Action: no defensive signal from bonds.
Corr SPY / TLT 60d
Stretch +1.82
Equity-bond regime
0.55 +5.16%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
60-day rolling correlation between SPY (equities) and TLT (long bonds). Deflation regime = negative correlation (bonds = haven, play their hedging role). Inflation regime = positive correlation (both fall together — risk parity under stress). Current reading: +0.55 — inflation regime active. Bonds and equities fall together, the traditional 60/40 no longer works as a hedge. Action: don't count on TLT to hedge SPY, look for other protections (gold, VIX calls, cash).

XLY / XLP
Stretch -0.30
Consumer confidence
1.3639 +2.74%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
XLY = consumer discretionary (cyclical, discretionary purchases), XLP = consumer staples (defensive, necessary purchases). When XLP beats XLY, the consumer is going into cautious mode — often a bearish divergence if SPX keeps rising. Current reading: Z=-0.30 — balanced discretionary/staples. No defensive rotation. Action: no sector signal.
IWM / SPY
Stretch +2.18
Risk appetite (small vs large)
0.3936 +1.24%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
IWM = Russell 2000 (small caps), SPY = S&P 500 (large caps). When small caps underperform strongly, it means breadth is dying — the rally is only carried by mega-caps. Classic end-of-cycle signal. Current reading: Z=+2.18 — small caps leading the market. Risk-on broadening, signal of a healthy bull market. Action: stay offensive, possible small cap exposure.
SMH / SPY
Stretch +2.01
Tech leadership
0.8261 +4.97%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
SMH = semis ETF (Nvidia, AMD, etc.), SPY = S&P 500. When semis dominate to the extreme, it's often an exhaustion signal (tech can't outperform indefinitely). When they break down, it's the end of the tech leadership cycle. Current reading: Z=+2.01 — semis dominating at the extreme. Unsustainable tech leadership level, rising mean-reversion pressure. Action: trim NVDA/AMD/SMH, beware of recent entrants.
SPY / RSP
Stretch +0.39
Cap-weighted vs equal-weighted
3.5173 +0.14%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
SPY = S&P 500 cap-weighted (mega-caps weigh enormously), RSP = S&P 500 equal-weighted (each stock 0.2%). When SPY outperforms RSP, mega-caps are hiding the weakness of the rest — dangerous concentration. Current reading: Z=+0.39 — broad market participation. No dangerous concentration. Action: no signal.
XLI / XLU
Stretch +1.51
Offensive vs defensive
3.9762 +3.12%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
XLI = industrials (cyclical pro-growth), XLU = utilities (defensive, haven). When XLU outperforms XLI, smart money seeks shelter in safe stocks — caution signal. Current reading: Z=+1.51 — cyclical confidence. Industrials leading utilities, growth optimism. Action: risk-on confirmed.

Greed gauge
1.9846 +4.97%

EV% = win_rate × (target−entry) − (1−win_rate) × (entry−stop). Tier = T1 highest conviction. 52w% = % of 52-week high. Entry/stop/target levels live on the Trade Desk →
How to read
Heat ranking: composite of RS (relative strength vs SPY), Momentum (price action 20-60d), Breadth (% stocks above SMA), Options flow, and Attention. Use top-5 as rotation focus.
Emerging industries: high velocity = screener scores accelerating this week vs prior baseline — potential early rotation signal.
Competitor themes: token frequency × recency across top finance Substacks — useful narrative context, not a trading signal.
Best R/R: only entries with an active screener leg (PULLBACK / BOTTOM / SMART_MONEY_SURGE etc.). EV% = probabilistic edge per trade. Cross-check with weekly chart before sizing.

Reading the table — Z-Score: number of standard deviations vs rolling 100d mean. |Z| > 2 = Kerberos entry threshold, |Z| > 2.5 = extreme (best R/R on mean-reversion). %B: position within Bollinger Bands (1.0 = upper band, 0 = lower band).

Aggregate reading — the mean of Z-scores gives a global regime indicator. Positive → cross-asset overbought market (Risk-On, watch reversal). Negative → oversold (Risk-Off, possible bottoming).
How to read
Persist = number of consecutive days above |Z| > 2.0 (Kerberos threshold). Persist ≥ 3d = validated signal (the divergence has become a regime, not a spike). Persist 1-2d = to watch. Persist 0d = dead signal. Sort on this column to find established regimes worth trading.
Glossary — 18 terms to understand the dashboard
Z-Score
Number of standard deviations vs the historical mean (rolling 100 days). Z=0 = normal level · |Z|>2 = stress · |Z|>2.5 = extreme.
Bollinger Bands
Bands drawn at ±2.2σ around a 100-day moving average. When the price exits the bands = statistically extreme signal.
Backwardation
Inverted curve: immediate fear exceeds far fear (e.g. VIX > VIX3M). Short-term panic signal.
Contango
Normal situation where far fear exceeds immediate fear. Extreme contango = complacency.
Half-life
Number of days required for a gap to the mean to halve. Measures the speed of mean-reversion.
Cointegration
Two assets that move together in the long term despite short-term fluctuations. Statistical basis of pair-trading.
Mean-reversion
Strategy betting that a gap to the mean will close. Inverse of momentum (which bets on continuation).
Breadth
Width of participation in the rally. Good breadth = many stocks rising. Bad breadth = only a few mega-caps pull the index.
Hedge ratio
Quantity of asset B to short to neutralize 1 long unit of asset A. Calculated via OLS regression on historical returns.
Lead-time
Delay between a signal's appearance and the event it predicts. Structural = 30-90d · stress = 5-30d · triggers = 1-10d.
Bonferroni
Statistical correction for multiple testing. When testing 100 signals, some seem valid by chance. Bonferroni divides the significance threshold by the number of tests to eliminate these false positives.
MCPT (Monte Carlo Permutation Test)
Robustness test: data is randomly shuffled 5000-10000 times to see if the signal appears by chance. If p < 0.05, the signal is probably real.
Walk-forward
Test where the model is trained on the first years (e.g. 2010-2020) and tested on the following years (2020-2025). Avoids in-sample overfitting.
Tail-hedge
Hedge against an extreme crash (-20% or more). Bought via deeply out-of-the-money puts. SKEW measures institutional demand for this type of insurance.
Mega-caps
The 7-10 largest market caps in the SPX (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, GOOGL, AMZN, META, TSLA). Weigh ~35% of the index.
Cap-weighted vs Equal-weighted
SPY = weighted by capitalization (mega-caps dominate). RSP = each stock weighs 0.2%. Comparing both reveals concentration.
Risk parity
Portfolio strategy that balances risk (not capital) across asset classes. Stressed when stocks/bonds correlations turn positive (inflation regime).
framework rule
Fundamental principle: a solo signal = noise, 3+ aligned signals = real edge. All panel signals are Bonferroni-validated over 15 years of SPY.
